title: North Star system acceptance report
description: Machine-readable North Star system acceptance. Recomputes coverage from the proof ledger (not from self-declared CSV coverage), detects missing/conflicting/paper-completion outcomes, verifies rollback and provenance, and FAILS CLOSED unless every North Star anchor and every high-level phase resolves to passing child proofs. This is a planning/acceptance DEFINITION artifact; it performs no product execution, deploys no schema, and writes no live data.

north_star_requirements.required_outcome: LifeOS is a living extension of David's intent.
Company, personal, development, and home form one coherent whole.
David can see and understand the whole.
David can direct it as one system.
LifeOS advances every domain without fragmenting the whole.

acceptance_contract.verification_gate: Acceptance recomputes coverage from proofs, detects missing or conflicting outcomes, verifies rollback and provenance, and fails closed unless the sum equals the North Star.
acceptance_contract.evaluation_rule: sum_equals_north_star := (every North Star anchor recomputed-resolved) AND (every high-level phase recomputed-resolved) AND (zero missing outcomes) AND (zero unresolved conflicting outcomes) AND (owner_execution_approval == open) AND (global_coverage_gate == open). acceptance_result := accept-release IF sum_equals_north_star ELSE fail-closed.
acceptance_contract.coverage_authority: A child counts as PASS only when its lifecycle_state == complete AND a proof-record file exists at proof_records/<task_id>.proof.json. Self-declared CSV coverage_status ('covered-high-level') is never accepted as a pass.

checks.name: fail_closed_unless_sum_equals_north_star
checks.gate_clause: fails closed unless the sum equals the North Star

north_star_anchors.required_outcome: LifeOS is a living extension of David's intent.
Company, personal, development, and home form one coherent whole.
David can see and understand the whole.
David can direct it as one system.
LifeOS advances every domain without fragmenting the whole.

high_level_phases.phase_title: All valid existing work preserved for consolidation
Peer capabilities physically consolidated into LifeOS
Unified memory compute network and learning substrate integrated
Financial and legal MVP1 operating domain completed
Company and development operating environments consolidated
Coherent LifeOS experience delivered across user interfaces
Whole LifeOS product proven and reproducibly released
LifeOS continuously improves under governed feedback

C1_coverage_recomputed_from_proofs.note: 0 of 5 anchors and 0 of 13 phases resolve to fully-passing child proofs; every anchor still carries draft (unproven) children and every LIFEOS phase anchor is itself Draft.

C2_missing_outcomes.anchor_proof_uri_note: All 5 North Star anchors cite proof_records/LIFEOS-000.proof.json, which does not exist on disk -> missing anchor-level proof.
C2_missing_outcomes.complete_without_proof_note: NBSOURCE-006 and NBVERIFY-030 are marked complete but have no proof_records/<id>.proof.json; their evidence lives in source extracts / a bundled proof and is not an independent proof record.
C2_missing_outcomes.verdict: MISSING OUTCOMES PRESENT -> coverage cannot be certified complete.

revision_superseded_not_conflict.rule: a '-v2' proof record supersedes its '-v1'; the acceptance treats it as a revision, not a divergent outcome.

rolled_back_terminal.rule: LPS-025 is a clean terminal rollback recorded in the ledger, not an unresolved conflict.

owner_conflict_on_durable_state.basis: STORE-001 assigns every durable macro-state class exactly one canonical owner (PostgreSQL+RuVector) with redb as transient-only; the single-durable-writer invariant forbids a second durable owner, so no owner conflict exists.
owner_conflict_on_durable_state.invariant: Single durable writer: PostgreSQL owns every durable macro-state class exactly once; redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class.

C3_conflicting_outcomes.verdict: No unresolved conflicting outcomes detected; detector ran and classified revisions/rollbacks/owner-authority explicitly.

flagged_anchors.verdict: downgraded-to-unresolved: coverage self-declared but child proofs incomplete
downgraded-to-unresolved: coverage self-declared but child proofs incomplete
downgraded-to-unresolved: coverage self-declared but child proofs incomplete
downgraded-to-unresolved: coverage self-declared but child proofs incomplete
downgraded-to-unresolved: coverage self-declared but child proofs incomplete

C4_paper_completion.rule: Goal forbids 'paper completion'. Any anchor that self-declares coverage while its child proofs are incomplete is downgraded to unresolved. All 5 anchors are currently paper-covered (families present, owner decision / source verification pending) and are NOT accepted as passing.
C4_paper_completion.verdict: 5/5 anchors downgraded from self-declared coverage to unresolved.

C5_rollback_verification.release001_rollback_plan: Restore the archived acceptance plan and report; execution remains closed.

executed_rollback_evidence.meaning: rollback path is not theoretical; it has been exercised and recorded.

C5_rollback_verification.verdict: PASS: 196/196 tasks carry a rollback_plan and an executed rollback is present in the ledger.

supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
C6_provenance_verification.adopted_capability_provenance: generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv + generated/recovery_adoption_map.csv (every adopted capability carries source commit + PR + CI receipt; ADOPT-001 proof).
C6_provenance_verification.verdict: PASS: provenance chain intact (foundational blueprint + supporting sources + adoption provenance + sha256 ledger/graph pins).

C7_fail_closed_evaluation.verdict: FAIL-CLOSED is the correct behavior: the sum does not equal the North Star (0/5 anchors, 0/13 phases resolved; missing anchor proof; owner execution closed; coverage gate closed). The acceptance refuses to certify release. Execution remains closed.

store_001_alignment.why_relevant: System acceptance must not certify a topology that silently reclassifies durable state. STORE-001 fixes the canonical owners (PostgreSQL+RuVector durable; redb transient; AgentDB per-agent cognition; envctl forward bridge/projection; nu_plugin CodeDB ingestion) so 'no capability loss' and 'no owner conflict' are checkable invariants, and any redb->PostgreSQL move is a verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover, never an assumption.
store_001_alignment.capability_loss_guard: Verified cutover: a class leaves redb-durable only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run against a disposable PostgreSQL for that class; never by assumption.
store_001_alignment.single_writer_invariant: Single durable writer: PostgreSQL owns every durable macro-state class exactly once; redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class.
